AR poisoning represents a paradigm shift for what a successful indiscriminate poisoning attack looks like, and raises the question of whether strong indiscriminate poisons need to be generated by surrogate networks for a given dataset.

To conduct poisoning attacks on neural networks, recent works have modiﬁed data to explicitly cause gradient vanishing [31] or to minimize the loss with respect to the input image [18]. Images perturbed with error-minimizing noises are a surprisingly good data poisoning attack.
